g.text: I'll create a comprehensive D2 diagram showing the RequestVote RPC sequence diagram with all the algorithm steps involved in the voting process

div.p: Step 3: Vote Decision Logic Rule 1: If args.Term < currentTerm → REJECT Rule 2: If args.Term > currentTerm:

ul.li: Persist, step down to Follower Rule 3: Check vote eligibility:
votedFor == "" OR votedFor == candidateId Rule 4: Check log up-to-date:

div.p: Grant vote if: eligible AND up-to-date
Step 5: Become Leader On majority (votes >= 3 of 5):
state = Leader initialize nextIndex[] = lastLogIndex + 1 initialize matchIndex[] = 0 send immediate heartbeats start heartbeat loop

ol.li: Persist Before Respond: Vote must be persisted to stable storage BEFORE setting VoteGranted=true
Quorum Intersection: Any two majorities overlap by at least one node, preventing double-voting
Log Up-to-Date: Term-first comparison ensures new leader has all committed entries

div.p: reply.VoteGranted = true persist() // Too late if crash here!
votedFor = candidateId persist() // FIRST reply.VoteGranted = true

g.text: This will illustrate the candidate sending RequestVote to followers, the vote granting logic, and the election completion flow
